passage.title: Toy Turn Cards
passage.text: Our group keeps a game shelf list near the blocks area. After reading, Noah reads it before cleanup time. The game shelf list has name tags for turn-taking at each play spot for the day's job. Our teacher asks us to put toys back in the correct place. Sometimes we compare today's turn tag with yesterday's turn tag and notice one small change. We like the game shelf list because it helps us play more peacefully.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the game shelf list is used.

questions.prompt: Where is the game shelf list?
questions.rationale: The first sentence says the game shelf list is near the blocks area.

questions.prompt: When does Noah use the game shelf list?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Noah uses it after reading.

questions.prompt: What does the game shelf list have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the game shelf list has turn tags.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
